opinion: There is no such thing as private property. It's the government's responsibility to equally distribute all goods; anyone who disagrees is an enemy of the state.
Eliminate private property entirely, cap all personal income at a state-defined living standard, and seize all excess wealth for compulsory redistribution through state-run welfare systems.
Impose extremely high marginal tax rates and aggressive wealth taxes on the rich to fund expansive government programs.
Significantly increase corporate and capital taxes while maintaining a highly progressive income tax to reduce inequality.
Maintain a progressive tax system with targeted credits and relief for working- and middle-class households.
Lower corporate and capital taxes to encourage investment, job creation, and economic growth.
Adopt a flat, low tax rate with minimal exemptions and sharply reduce the scope of government taxation.
Declare all compulsory taxation illegitimate, abolish income taxes and the IRS entirely, and reject the government’s authority to tax.
Taxation is theft, and any attempt to impose taxes should be met with armed resistance and lethal action for the offending party.
